#----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# libs/Einsums/Python — Python integration support headers and the
# autogenerated binding aggregator.
#
# This directory is *not* a regular einsums_add_module() call: it ships
# only a header (Annotations.hpp) plus a hand-written runtime adapter
# that the autogen pipeline writes binding code against.
#
# Annotations.hpp is made globally reachable via einsums_public_flags
# (see cmake/Einsums_CompilerFlagsTargets.cmake), so every module that
# uses the EINSUMS_PYBIND_* macros can include it without an explicit
# MODULE_DEPENDENCIES entry.
#
# Activation:
#   - The annotations header is always available — its macros expand to
#     nothing on non-Clang compilers, so the include is a free no-op.
#   - The autogen pipeline (per-module Python extensions named
#     ``PyEinsums_<modulename>``) is only built when EINSUMS_PYBIND_AUTOGEN=ON
#     and EINSUMS_BUILD_PYTHON is set, since it depends on pybind11 and
#     on the einsums-pybind code generator.

if(NOT EINSUMS_BUILD_PYTHON)
    return()
endif()
if(NOT EINSUMS_PYBIND_AUTOGEN)
    return()
endif()

# einsums-pybind is built under tools/einsums-pybind. The root CMakeLists
# guards add_subdirectory on the same option, so the target should exist
# by the time we reach here. Bail with a clear message if it doesn't.
if(NOT TARGET einsums-pybind)
    message(FATAL_ERROR
        "EINSUMS_PYBIND_AUTOGEN=ON but the einsums-pybind target is not defined. "
        "Make sure tools/einsums-pybind is added before libs/."
    )
endif()

if(NOT TARGET pybind11::module)
    message(FATAL_ERROR "EINSUMS_PYBIND_AUTOGEN=ON requires pybind11.")
endif()

# Phase 5 ships per-module Python extensions: each einsums_add_module that
# passes ``PYBIND`` produces a target ``PyEinsums_<modulename>`` linking
# the codegen output. Phase 7 will add a hand-written EinsumAdapter.cpp
# here for the einsum runtime adapter.
